question: What are the common disorientations during UAS operation, and how are they overcome?

options.text: In hazy weather, the drone actually appears nearer than it actually is - Do not get tricked by your own eyes; use the app as a reference to see the distance of the drone from yourself.
Do not use surrounding visual reference points to navigate the UAS - trees, buildings, roads or canals.
Apply sudden head and hand movements in flight, particularly when manoeuvring - too little movement will cause different illusions.
Ensure best practices such as using app height indications as a reference if an illusion occurs - In hazy weather, the drone actually appears further than it actually is.
